concepts.atomicConcept: Fluently Add and Subtract Multi-Digit Decimals
keySkills: Add and subtract multi-digit numbers involving decimals using the standard algorithm

concepts.atomicConcept: Fluently Multiply Multi-Digit Decimals
keySkills: Multiply multi-digit numbers involving decimals using the standard algorithm

concepts.atomicConcept: Fluently Divide Multi-Digit Whole Numbers (Long Division)
keySkills: Divide multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm for long division

concepts.atomicConcept: Fluently Divide Multi-Digit Decimals
keySkills: Divide multi-digit decimals using the standard algorithm

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Fraction Division (Conceptually)
keySkills: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions using visual models (e.g., how many 3/4s are in 1/2)

concepts.atomicConcept: Computing Quotients of Fractions by Fractions (Algorithm)
keySkills: Apply the algorithm (invert and multiply) to divide fractions by fractions

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Word Problems Involving Fraction Division
keySkills: Use fraction division to solve real-world problems involving lengths, areas, and quantities

concepts.atomicConcept: Finding the Greatest Common Factor (GCF) of Two Whole Numbers
keySkills: Find the GCF of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100

concepts.atomicConcept: Finding the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of Two Whole Numbers
keySkills: Find the LCM of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12

concepts.atomicConcept: Using GCF and LCM to Rewrite Expressions (Distributive Property, Common Denominators)
keySkills: Use the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers with a common factor

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Positive and Negative Numbers in Context
keySkills: Use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world contexts (e.g., temperature, elevation)

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Integers and Opposites
keySkills: Define integers
Understand that opposite numbers are equidistant from zero on a number line

concepts.atomicConcept: Plotting Rational Numbers on a Number Line and Coordinate Plane
keySkills: Plot rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line and in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Absolute Value of Rational Numbers
keySkills: Define absolute value as distance from zero
Interpret absolute value in real-world situations

concepts.atomicConcept: Comparing and Ordering Rational Numbers
keySkills: Use inequality symbols (<, >, =) to compare rational numbers
Order rational numbers on a number line

concepts.atomicConcept: Graphing Points in All Four Quadrants of the Coordinate Plane
keySkills: Plot points (x, y) in all four quadrants
Understand the meaning of coordinates in context

concepts.atomicConcept: Finding Distances Between Points in the Coordinate Plane
keySkills: Find distances between points with the same first or second coordinate by applying absolute value

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Ratios (Definition and Different Forms)
keySkills: Define a ratio as a comparison of two quantities
Write ratios in fraction form, with a colon, or with the word "to"

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Rates and Unit Rates
keySkills: Define a rate as a ratio comparing quantities with different units
Define a unit rate as a rate with a denominator of 1

concepts.atomicConcept: Calculating Unit Rates (from Ratios and Rates)
keySkills: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of whole numbers, fractions, and decimals

concepts.atomicConcept: Using Ratio Reasoning in Tables and Tape Diagrams
keySkills: Use tables of equivalent ratios or tape diagrams to solve ratio problems

concepts.atomicConcept: Using Ratio Reasoning with Double Number Lines and Equations
keySkills: Use double number line diagrams or write simple equations to solve ratio problems

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Real-World Ratio Problems
keySkills: Apply ratio reasoning to solve various real-world problems (e.g., mixing paint colors, calculating ingredients)

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Percentages as Rates Per 100
keySkills: Define a percentage as a rate per 100
Understand percent as "per hundred"

concepts.atomicConcept: Finding a Percent of a Quantity
keySkills: Calculate a given percent of a number (e.g., find 30% of 200)

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Percentage Problems (Finding the Whole or the Part)
keySkills: Solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent
Find what percent one number is of another

concepts.atomicConcept: Converting Between Fractions, Decimals, and Percentages
keySkills: Convert between fraction, decimal, and percent forms of numbers

concepts.atomicConcept: Using Ratio and Rate Reasoning to Solve Measurement Problems
keySkills: Apply ratio and rate reasoning to convert measurement units by multiplying or dividing by ratios

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Numerical Expressions with Whole-Number Exponents
keySkills: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents (e.g., 3² + 5)

concepts.atomicConcept: Writing Algebraic Expressions from Verbal Descriptions
keySkills: Translate verbal phrases into algebraic expressions (e.g., "the sum of 5 and a number x")

concepts.atomicConcept: Identifying Parts of an Algebraic Expression
keySkills: Identify terms, factors, coefficients, and exponents within an expression

concepts.atomicConcept: Evaluating Algebraic Expressions
keySkills: Substitute given values for variables and evaluate expressions (e.g., if x=3, find 2x+1)

concepts.atomicConcept: Applying the Distributive Property to Generate Equivalent Expressions
keySkills: Use the distributive property to rewrite expressions (e.g., 3(x+2) = 3x+6)

concepts.atomicConcept: Combining Like Terms to Generate Equivalent Expressions
keySkills: Identify like terms
Combine like terms to simplify expressions (e.g., 2x + 3y + 5x = 7x + 3y)

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding What it Means to Solve an Equation or Inequality
keySkills: Understand that solutions make the equation/inequality true
Use substitution to check solutions

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving One-Step Equations with Addition or Subtraction (x+p=q)
keySkills: Solve equations of the form x+p=q using inverse operations (e.g., x+5=12)

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving One-Step Equations with Multiplication or Division (px=q)
keySkills: Solve equations of the form px=q using inverse operations (e.g., 3x=15)

concepts.atomicConcept: Writing Equations to Solve Real-World Problems
keySkills: Translate word problems into one-step equations (e.g., "If I have 10 apples and give away x, I have 3 left, write the equation")

concepts.atomicConcept: Writing Inequalities to Represent Constraints (x>c, x<c, etc.)
keySkills: Write inequalities to express conditions in real-world situations (e.g., "speed limit is 55 mph, so s ≤ 55")

concepts.atomicConcept: Representing Solutions of Inequalities on Number Lines
keySkills: Graph the solution set of an inequality (e.g., x > 2) on a number line

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Real-World Problems Involving Linear Inequalities
keySkills: Construct inequalities from word problems (e.g., "At most $15", "at least $12")

concepts.atomicConcept: Using Variables to Represent Two Quantities in a Real-World Relationship
keySkills: Identify dependent and independent variables in a real-world context
Represent them with variables

concepts.atomicConcept: Writing Equations to Express Relationships Between Dependent and Independent Variables
keySkills: Write an equation to represent a relationship between two quantities (e.g., distance = rate × time)

concepts.atomicConcept: Analyzing Relationships Between Dependent and Independent Variables Using Graphs and Tables
keySkills: Use graphs and tables to analyze how a change in the independent variable affects the dependent variable

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Area of Triangles
keySkills: Find the area of triangles by composing into rectangles or decomposing into right triangles.
Use the formula A = ½bh to calculate area.

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Area of Quadrilaterals (Parallelograms)
keySkills: Find the area of parallelograms by composing into rectangles or decomposing.
Use the formula A = bh to calculate area.

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Area of Quadrilaterals (Trapezoids)
keySkills: Find the area of trapezoids by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and rectangles.
Use the formula A = ½(b₁+b₂)h to calculate area.

concepts.atomicConcept: Finding Areas of Other Polygons (Composing and Decomposing)
keySkills: Find areas of complex polygons by decomposing them into triangles, rectangles, and other known shapes.
Recompose shapes to form new polygons with known area formulas.

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Polyhedra and Nets
keySkills: Define polyhedra as three-dimensional figures composed of polygons
Draw and analyze nets of cubes, prisms, and pyramids.

concepts.atomicConcept: Calculating Surface Area of 3D Figures using Nets
keySkills: Find the surface area of cubes and right prisms using their nets.
Calculate the area of each face and sum them.

concepts.atomicConcept: Calculating Surface Area of Right Pyramids using Nets
keySkills: Find the surface area of right pyramids using their nets, by calculating the area of the base and all triangular faces.

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Volume of Right Rectangular Prisms
keySkills: Understand that the volume of a right rectangular prism is found by packing it with unit cubes.

concepts.atomicConcept: Calculating Volume of Right Rectangular Prisms with Whole-Number Edge Lengths
keySkills: Apply the formula V=lwh or V=Bh to calculate the volume of prisms with whole-number dimensions.

concepts.atomicConcept: Calculating Volume of Right Rectangular Prisms with Fractional Edge Lengths
keySkills: Calculate the volume of right rectangular prisms with fractional side lengths by packing with fractional unit cubes.
Apply the formulas V=lwh and V=Bh.

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Real-World Problems Involving Area
keySkills: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and other polygons.

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Real-World Problems Involving Surface Area
keySkills: Solve real-world problems involving surface area of cubes, prisms, and pyramids (e.g., painting a room, wrapping a gift).

concepts.atomicConcept: Solving Real-World Problems Involving Volume
keySkills: Solve real-world problems involving volume of right rectangular prisms (e.g., finding capacity of a box, filling a container).

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Statistical Questions
keySkills: Define a statistical question (one that anticipates variability in data)
Differentiate from non-statistical questions

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Data Distributions and Data Sets
keySkills: Recognize a data set as a collection of numerical or categorical data
Understand variability in data

concepts.atomicConcept: Representing Data on Dot Plots and Histograms
keySkills: Create dot plots and histograms to display numerical data
Interpret basic features of these plots

concepts.atomicConcept: Representing Data on Box Plots (Box-and-Whisker Plots)
keySkills: Create box plots to display numerical data
Identify five-number summary (min, Q1, median, Q3, max)

concepts.atomicConcept: Summarizing Data Distributions: Understanding Center (Mean, Median, Mode)
keySkills: Calculate mean, median, and mode for a numerical data set
Interpret each as a measure of center

concepts.atomicConcept: Summarizing Data Distributions: Understanding Variability (Range, IQR)
keySkills: Calculate range and interquartile range (IQR) for a numerical data set
Interpret as measures of variability

concepts.atomicConcept: Understanding Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD)
keySkills: Calculate MAD for a numerical data set as a measure of variation
Interpret what it means about typical deviation from the mean

concepts.atomicConcept: Describing the Overall Shape of Data Distributions (Symmetry, Skew, Peaks)
keySkills: Describe data distributions by their shape (e.g., symmetric, skewed left/right, peaks)
Identify clusters and gaps

concepts.atomicConcept: Identifying Outliers in Data Distributions
keySkills: Recognize outliers as data points significantly different from others
Explain their potential impact on measures of center and variability

concepts.atomicConcept: Summarizing Numerical Data Sets in Relation to their Context (Part 1)
keySkills: Report the number of observations
Describe the context that generated the data

concepts.atomicConcept: Summarizing Numerical Data Sets in Relation to their Context (Part 2)
keySkills: Relate the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context
